exercises.prompt: What units do you combine to make velocity? (Hint: distance/time). What about acceleration? (Hint: velocity/time, or distance/time²)
exercises.solution: Velocity = m/s (distance unit divided by time unit). Acceleration = m/s² (velocity unit divided by time). Speed = m/s. Force = kg⋅m/s². Energy = kg⋅m²/s². Power = kg⋅m²/s³. Combine the base units!

exercises.prompt: For each: Speed, Force, Energy, Power—write what base units combine to make it.
exercises.solution: Velocity = m/s (distance unit divided by time unit). Acceleration = m/s² (velocity unit divided by time). Speed = m/s. Force = kg⋅m/s². Energy = kg⋅m²/s². Power = kg⋅m²/s³. Combine the base units!
